summary: Whitney Wolfe Herd discusses the founding inspiration behind Bumble, which empowers women to make the first move in dating to modernize outdated gender norms. She also reflects on the intense, workaholic lifestyle required to build the company, noting the personal sacrifices she made.

principles.statement: Women should be empowered to make the first move in dating, just as they do in their professional lives.
principles.rationale: It is an outdated double standard that women can achieve their dream jobs but are still expected to sit and wait for men to initiate romantic interactions. Men also experience fatigue from always having to make the first move.
principles.source_quote: Why is there this standard that, as a woman, you can get your dream job but you can't talk to a guy first? Let's make dating feel more modern.

mental_models.name: The Sadie Hawkins Model
mental_models.description: Using the dynamic of a Sadie Hawkins school dance—where women ask men to be their date—as a structural mechanism to flip traditional gender roles in digital dating.
mental_models.example: Designing Bumble so that women must initiate the conversation, relieving men of the pressure to always make the first move and empowering women.

signature_quotes.text: Why is there this standard that, as a woman, you can get your dream job but you can't talk to a guy first? Let's make dating feel more modern.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining the rationale behind Bumble's women-make-the-first-move mechanic.

signature_quotes.text: I know guys get sick of making the first move all the time. Why does a girl feel like she should sit and wait around?
signature_quotes.context: Discussing how traditional dating norms negatively impact both men and women.

signature_quotes.text: I get no downtime. I don't get a weekend, I haven't lived like a twenty-something since I started Bumble in 2014.
signature_quotes.context: Reflecting on the intense workaholic lifestyle of building a startup.

anti_patterns.description: Adhering to traditional dating standards where women wait for men to initiate.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It creates an outdated dynamic that contradicts women's empowerment in other areas of life (like careers) and places an exhausting burden on men to always make the first move.

anti_patterns.description: Waking up every two hours during the night to check your inbox.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It completely eliminates downtime and weekends, leading to an unsustainable workaholic lifestyle that consumes your personal life.
